offer: Never Miss a Call — AI Receptionist for local service businesses
method: 10-agent research+adversarial-verify workflow (run wf_9efe2b46-1f1), 158 live web fetches, 2026-07-09

suppliers.label: My AI Front Desk
suppliers.findings:
1 pricing: End-customer plans — Basic $20/mo ($16 annual), Business-in-a-Box $99/mo ($79 annual, incl. 200 voice min), Partner/Enterprise custom; annual = 20% off, billed monthly or annually [high] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/pricing)
2 white_label/reseller: REAL full white-label reseller program (not affiliate) — own domain, logo, colors; "your clients never see Frontdesk"; separate reseller vs client dashboards; feature toggles per client [high] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/white-label)
3 phone numbers: BOTH US and Canada provisionable — Canadian local numbers confirmed (area codes 416 Toronto, 249, 807 Ontario), US location-based numbers; reseller picks country per client via Twilio [high] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/reseller-tutorials/configuring-phone-numbers-for-clients-in-my-ai-frontdesk)
4 call forwarding + porting: YES both — use AI number as main line OR forward existing office line (ring/second threshold configurable); port/import existing numbers incl. own Twilio numbers into subaccounts [high] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/tutorials/how-to-integrate-your-business-phone-with-my-ai-front-desk-receptionist)
5 calendar booking: Google Calendar + Calendly confirmed (native + via Zapier); Cal.com not explicitly named — likely reachable via Zapier only [medium] (https://llms.myaifrontdesk.com/white-label-reseller-agencies)
6 SMS/email lead summary: SMS follow-ups included (400/mo Business); email drafts included (500/mo Business) — post-call SMS/email summary supported, exact "summary" wording not verbatim-confirmed [medium] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/pricing)
7 CRM/Sheets/Zapier/webhooks: YES — 6,000+ Zapier integrations; HubSpot, Salesforce, Attio named; Google Sheets reachable via Zapier; webhooks implied through Zapier [high] (https://llms.myaifrontdesk.com/white-label-reseller-agencies)
8 Stripe / client sub-account billing: YES — one-click Stripe connect, WE bill clients directly, unlimited client subaccounts, set custom pricing + minute allocations, subscriptions/trials/usage fees automated [high] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/white-label)
9 setup time: ~5 min to deploy one receptionist; "launch in 24 hours"; resellers report sign-up to first branded saleable product in ~7 days — fits 7-day launch [high] (https://llms.myaifrontdesk.com/white-label-reseller-agencies)
10 demo quality: Strong — public live demo line +1 (864) 619-0619; free plan no credit card + 10 test credits to build/test your own agent; book CSM call [high] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/tutorials/how-to-build-and-test-your-ai-receptionist-for-free)
11 cancellation risk: Marketing says "cancel anytime / no contracts," and reseller program claims no minimums/lock-in — BUT third-party reviews report annual-plan refund DENIALS, $299 charge on day 8 of a 7-day trial, billing continuing after cancel request, refusals citing ToS, email-only support [medium] (https://www.trustpilot.com/review/www.myaifrontdesk.com)
12 hidden per-minute/overage: REAL and material — voice overage 25 credits = $0.25/min after included minutes (Enterprise as low as $0.07/min); Business auto-reloads credits at $10/1000; ~1,000 min ≈ $299 effective — dangerous margin risk for call-heavy plumbers/HVAC [high] (https://autocalls.ai/article/my-ai-front-desk-review)
13 resell under OUR brand: FULL WHITE-LABEL (not affiliate/referral) — SourceA/Noetfield brand on domain/logo/emails/caller experience, reseller portal, Stripe rebilling, unlimited subaccounts; wholesale price is APPLY-BASED / sales-gated (UNKNOWN exact — unofficial figures conflict: ~$54.99/receptionist marketing vs ~$500/mo third-party) [medium] (https://www.myaifrontdesk.com/white-label)
suppliers.research_red_flags: 1) Wholesale/reseller price is SALES-GATED (apply-based) — no official number published; unofficial figures conflict badly (~$54.99/receptionist in marketing blogs vs ~$500/mo minimum per third-party), so true margin is UNKNOWN until we apply. 2) Per-minute overage economics are the biggest risk for OUR verticals: plumbers/HVAC/law are high-call-volume; at $0.25/min overage a busy client blows past 200 included minutes fast, crushing our margin unless we price minute packages carefully. 3) Third-party cancellation/refund complaints (annual-plan refund denials, day-8 trial charge of $299, billing after cancel, refusals citing ToS, email-only support) are a REPUTATION contagion risk — under white-label those support failures land on the SourceA/Noetfield brand, not Frontdesk. 4) Trustpilot page blocked WebFetch (403); cancellation specifics came from search-engine snippets of that page, not a direct read — treat as medium confidence. 5) Cal.com and true native webhooks not verbatim-confirmed (Zapier-mediated). 6) Vendor is mid-rebrand (My AI Front Desk -> Frontdesk) — brand/URL churn to monitor.

verify.summary: Adversarial result: the white-label is REAL and full (verified — own domain/logo/colors, "your clients never see Frontdesk," Stripe rebilling of your own clients, reseller vs client dashboards; it is NOT an affiliate/referral program mislabeled). END-CUSTOMER pricing is genuinely published (Basic $20/$16, Business-in-a-Box $99/$79 incl. 200 voice min, Partner/Enterprise custom). BUT the load-bearing number for a reseller — the WHOLESALE price — is genuinely sales-gated: neither the white-label page, the reseller-registration page, nor even the vendor's own blog titled "Transparent White Label Pricing Revealed" states a wholesale figure. The circulating ~$54.99/receptionist is vendor-marketing/competitor paraphrase, not a committed quote; true margin is UNKNOWN until you apply. Three deal-breaker claims were REFUTED at the primary source (the Terms of Service, verified directly): (1) "Charge $49 or $999, totally up to you" is contradicted by a Minimum Advertised Price (MAP) clause — "Resellers must comply with any MAP policy set by AI Front Desk, Inc.," changeable "at any time, with or without notice" — so pricing autonomy has a vendor-controlled floor; (2) "no lock-in / cancel anytime" is undercut by a license that is "revocable at any time, for any reason, at [vendor's] sole discretion" plus an absolute right to terminate any reseller "for any reason or no reason, with or without notice" with the vendor "not liable for any losses" — an inverse lock-in that can wipe out your SourceA/Noetfield-branded book overnight; (3) the written refund policy is "Refunds are generally not provided" with no annual-plan carve-out, corroborating independent complaints (Trustpilot/Serchen/welco: $299 charged after a 7-day trial, a ~$1,428 annual plan refund denied citing ToS, billing continuing after cancel, email-only support). The per-minute overage risk is confirmed and material: $0.25/min after only 200 included minutes (~7 min/day); ~1,000 min ≈ $299 effective; auto-reload $10/1,000 credits can silently accumulate; the "$0.07/min" is Enterprise/volume-gated only. New gaps found: no published HIPAA/SOC2/GDPR/TCPA compliance (blocks regulated legal/medical verticals), outbound capped at 20 calls/day even on paid plans, data retention capped at last 200 logs below Enterprise, no WhatsApp. Technical deploy is ~5 min per receptionist, but reseller access is application/approval-gated with unknown turnaround, so a 7-day branded launch is plausible-but-gated (maybe, not yes). Net: capability is real; the economics (wholesale price, MAP floor) and the contract (revocable license, no-liability termination, no-refund policy) are the actual risk, and the most decision-relevant numbers remain unverifiable until you apply.
red_flags: Reseller WHOLESALE price is genuinely sales-gated — not published on the white-label page, the reseller-registration page, or even the vendor's own 'Transparent White Label Pricing Revealed' blog; the ~$54.99/receptionist figure is marketing/competitor paraphrase, NOT a committed quote, so true margin is UNKNOWN until you apply.

ToS MAP (Minimum Advertised Price) clause CONTRADICTS the 'Charge $49 or $999, totally up to you' pricing-autonomy marketing: 'Resellers must comply with any MAP policy set by AI Front Desk, Inc.' and the vendor can change it 'at any time, with or without notice' — pricing has a vendor-controlled floor.

White-label license is REVOCABLE 'at any time, for any reason, at [vendor's] sole discretion'; vendor can terminate any reseller 'for any reason or no reason, with or without notice' and is 'not liable for any losses' — inverse lock-in that can kill a SourceA/Noetfield-branded client book overnight with no recourse.

Written refund policy (ToS Sec. 13): 'Refunds are generally not provided,' no annual-plan carve-out — corroborated by independent complaints (Trustpilot/Serchen/welco): $299 charged after a 7-day trial, ~$1,428 annual refund denied citing ToS, billing continued after cancel, email-only support. Under white-label these failures hit YOUR brand.

Per-minute overage $0.25/min (25 credits) after only 200 included min (~7 min/day); ~1,000 min ≈ $299 effective; auto-reload $10/1,000 credits accumulates silently; the '$0.07/min' rate is Enterprise/volume-gated only — real margin killer for call-heavy plumbers/HVAC/law.
No published HIPAA/SOC2/GDPR/TCPA compliance — blocks regulated legal/medical/financial verticals and undermines reselling to them (per competitor Trillet, unrebutted by vendor).

Feature ceilings: outbound capped at 20 calls/day even on paid plans (no lead-reactivation at scale), no WhatsApp channel, data retention capped at last 200 logs below Enterprise — limits what you can credibly sell.

'No minimums' is inconsistent: a vendor reseller blog says start with 'just five accounts,' implying a floor; reselling lifetime access for a one-time fee is prohibited without written approval; resellers 'shall not modify core functionality' — reconcile before signing.
Reseller access is application/approval-gated with unknown turnaround — 7-day branded launch is plausible but NOT guaranteed; approval speed and wholesale terms are only revealed after you apply.

Vendor is mid-rebrand (My AI Front Desk -> Frontdesk) with brand/URL churn; Trustpilot direct read was blocked (403) and the review sample is thin (~15 reviews) — cancellation/refund specifics are consistent across sources but remain medium-confidence.

suppliers.label: OnCallClerk
suppliers.findings:
1 pricing: Public tiers — Free Starter ($0), Entry $29/mo, full-feature plans ~$99–$299/mo; annual billing saves 20% (~$23.20/mo entry); 14-day free trial, no setup fees, no contracts [high] (https://oncallclerk.com/pricing)
2 white_label/reseller: Real, genuine program — three tiers: Referral (affiliate), Co-branded, and full White-Label ("Your logo. Your domain. Your pricing. Your clients never know we exist.") with custom domain+SSL, branded call logs, centralized multi-client dashboard [high] (https://oncallclerk.com/white-label)
3 phone_numbers_CA_and_US: US CONFIRMED (local numbers every area code + toll-free); UK "rolling out." Canadian numbers NOT mentioned on any page read — unconfirmed and a real gap since ~half the target buyers are Canadian [medium] (https://oncallclerk.com/ | https://oncallclerk.com/compare/goodcall-alternative)
4 call_forwarding_porting: CONFIRMED — port-in ("port-your-own") + conditional call forwarding of existing line; AI does warm-transfer (with context brief) or cold-transfer to any number, rules by call type/time/request [high] (https://oncallclerk.com/phone-answering-service)
5 calendar_booking: Google Calendar AND Outlook confirmed with live availability checks + event creation; Calendly and Cal.com NOT explicitly named on pages read [high for Google/Outlook, unknown for Calendly/Cal.com] (https://oncallclerk.com/compare/goodcall-alternative)
6 sms_email_lead_summary: CONFIRMED — "Caller details, their inquiry, and any actions taken are sent to you via email, SMS, or both"; transcripts/summaries exportable to .txt/CSV [high] (https://oncallclerk.com/phone-answering-service)
7 crm_sheets_zapier_webhooks: Native HubSpot, Slack, Google, and Zapier; webhooks CONFIRMED ("triggered via webhooks for custom workflows"); CRM push confirmed. Google Sheets NOT explicitly named [high for Zapier/webhooks/HubSpot; unknown for Sheets] (https://oncallclerk.com/compare/goodcall-alternative | https://oncallclerk.com/phone-answering-service)
8 stripe_client_billing: White-label model states clients "pay you directly… you invoice them however you like" — WE own end-client billing (our own Stripe/invoicing). No evidence of a built-in Stripe sub-account/pass-through billing engine provided by the platform [medium] (https://oncallclerk.com/white-label)
9 setup_time: Very fast — marketing claims "5-minute setup," "live in under an hour," "set up in an afternoon" for a single client agent [high] (https://oncallclerk.com/)
10 demo_quality: Strong — live 24/7 demo line (888) 885-5251, a 30-second demo video, and ready-made industry-specific demo pages for white-label partners [high] (https://oncallclerk.com/)
11 cancellation_risk: Marketing says "No contracts," cancel/upgrade/downgrade anytime from dashboard (effective next cycle); monthly or annual (annual −20%). NO third-party reviews found (no Trustpilot/Reddit/G2; Capterra AU listing has zero ratings) and no published refund policy — cancellation/support experience is UNVERIFIED by any independent source [medium] (https://oncallclerk.com/pricing | https://www.capterra.com.au/software/1090145/OnCallClerk)
12 hidden_per_minute_cost: A per-minute overage DOES exist ("you only pay for calls we actually answer"; overage billed per-minute / "simple per-minute overage") but the actual rate is NOT published anywhere — UNKNOWN, effectively sales-gated; a real margin risk for resale, and no third-party data to benchmark it [medium] (https://oncallclerk.com/pricing | https://oncallclerk.com/compare/goodcall-alternative)
13 resell_under_our_brand: FULL WHITE-LABEL is offered (not merely affiliate) — our brand (SourceA/Noetfield), custom domain, no OnCallClerk mention client-side; also lighter Referral (affiliate) and Co-branded tiers. Wholesale cited ~$20–$50/client/mo depending on volume/features (from their blog, not a signed rate card) [high that full white-label exists; medium on exact wholesale] (https://oncallclerk.com/white-label | https://oncallclerk.com/blog/how-resellers-make-money-ai-receptionists)
suppliers.research_red_flags: 1) CANADA GAP: Canadian phone-number provisioning is not confirmed anywhere — site only cites US numbers plus UK "rolling out." Since ~half our target buyers are Canadian plumbers/HVAC/dentists/salons/law offices, this must be verified with the vendor BEFORE launch; if CA numbers aren't available, the offer is half-dead in-market. 2) UNDISCLOSED PER-MINUTE OVERAGE: overage exists but the rate is never published — direct threat to resale margins; get the wholesale rate card and overage rate in writing. 3) ZERO INDEPENDENT REVIEWS: no Trustpilot, Reddit, G2 presence; Capterra AU listing has no ratings — reliability, uptime, support quality, and real cancellation experience are entirely unverified (vendor appears young/low-footprint, possibly UK-registered entity). 4) Reseller economics ($20–$50/client wholesale, "no minimums") come from the vendor's own blog, not a signed agreement — terms, minimums, and lock-in are undocumented. 5) Integration gaps for our niche: Calendly/Cal.com and Google Sheets are NOT explicitly supported (only Google Calendar/Outlook, HubSpot, Slack, Zapier, webhooks); no published refund policy.

verify.summary: Adversarial re-verification against the LIVE site (pricing, white-label, terms, blog, homepage, goodcall-compare all fetched independently and returned distinct coherent content, so the site is genuinely live and content-complete). The three core POSITIVE claims survived every attempt to refute them: (a) pricing is REAL published numbers, not sales-gated or guessed — Free $0, Entry $29/mo, full-feature $99-$299/mo, annual -20%, 14-day trial, no setup fees, no contracts; (b) white-label is FULL and end-to-end, not affiliate mislabeled — custom domain ai.youragency.com + auto-SSL, your logo/branding/pricing, "clients never visit oncallclerk.com / never know we exist"; (c) we CAN bill our own clients at our own price under our brand — "They pay you directly. You set the price," with no anti-resale or price-control clause found in /white-label or /terms. TWO corrections to the finder: it claimed "no published refund policy" — FALSE, /terms publishes a NO-refund policy ("no refunds for partial months or unused services"), which is worse for the buyer; and its "possibly UK entity" guess is unresolved — /terms invokes California governing law while the compare page cites a "UK-registered legal entity," and no named legal entity appears anywhere (opaque). The deal-breaker red flags could NOT be refuted and several are confirmed hard: Canada number provisioning is absent everywhere (only "US and UK numbers, UK rolling out"), the per-minute overage rate is published nowhere (pricing + terms both only say "additional charges may apply / per-minute rate"), and there is ZERO independent third-party signal (Capterra AU literally "Rated by 0 users"; nothing real on G2/Trustpilot/Reddit; only auto-generated AI-tool directory listings). The domain was created 2025-07-04 (~1 year old) via Amazon Registrar with WHOIS privacy — a young, low-footprint vendor whose uptime, support quality, and cancellation experience are entirely unverifiable. Net: the platform advertises exactly what the finder said and the white-label/resale model is real, but this is a thin-evidence purchase — safe to trial for a single US client, not safe to build a Canadian-heavy resale book on without written wholesale + overage rates and CA-number confirmation from the vendor first.
red_flags: CANADA GAP CONFIRMED: site lists only 'US and UK numbers, UK rolling out' — no Canadian phone-number provisioning anywhere. Blocks ~half the target (Canadian) buyers; must be confirmed in writing with vendor BEFORE any Canada-facing launch.

PER-MINUTE OVERAGE RATE UNDISCLOSED: both /pricing and /terms only say 'per-minute rate' / 'additional charges may apply for extra call minutes' — no dollar figure published anywhere. Direct, unquantifiable threat to resale margins; get the exact overage rate in writing.

ZERO INDEPENDENT REVIEWS/FOOTPRINT: Capterra AU shows 'Rated by 0 users'; no G2/Trustpilot/Reddit presence; only auto-generated AI-directory listings (toolhunt, theresanaiforthat, slashdot, sourceforge). Reliability, uptime, support, and real cancellation experience are entirely unverified.

VERY YOUNG / OPAQUE VENDOR: domain created 2025-07-04 (~1 yr old) via Amazon Registrar with WHOIS privacy; no named legal entity; conflicting jurisdiction signals (Terms = California governing law vs compare page citing a 'UK-registered legal entity').

NO-REFUND POLICY IS PUBLISHED (correction to finder): /terms states 'We do not provide refunds for partial months or unused services.' Combined with prepaid monthly/annual billing, errors are non-recoverable — worse than the finder's 'no policy found' framing.

RESELLER ECONOMICS ARE UNSIGNED MARKETING MATH: wholesale '$20-$50/client' appears only in a blog and as '80%+ margin' examples on /white-label; no rate card, no minimums, no reseller agreement published. Terms, lock-in, and true wholesale must be obtained in a signed agreement.
INTEGRATION GAPS FOR THE NICHE: Calendly/Cal.com and Google Sheets are NOT explicitly supported — only Google Calendar/Outlook, HubSpot, Slack, Zapier, and webhooks are named.

7-DAY LAUNCH IS US-ONLY AND UNPROVEN: marketing '5-minute setup / live in under an hour' plausibly supports a single US client self-serve on the 14-day trial, but is blocked for Canadian clients and rests on real-world provisioning/support that no independent source has validated.

suppliers.label: ai-receptionist.com
suppliers.vendor: AI Receptionist (ai-receptionist.com), operated by 0x4d2 LLC, 1209 Mountain Road Place NE STE N, Albuquerque, NM 87110 USA. Founders Tim Molter & Alex Nugent (also of Knowm Inc., a Santa Fe neuromorphic-computing firm). Support 1-505-600-6295.
suppliers.findings:
1 pricing: 4 self-serve plans, monthly or ~10-mo-priced annual — Essential $14/mo ($140/yr, 60 min), Starter $39/mo ($399/yr, 60 min), Solopreneur $99/mo ($999/yr, 200 min), Business $199/mo ($1999/yr, 400 min); 3-day free trial, "no setup fees and no contracts"; Enterprise custom (unpriced) [high] (https://ai-receptionist.com/pricing/)
2 white_label/reseller: NONE — /white-label/, /reseller/, /partners/ all HTTP 404; help center has no reseller/white-label/agency article; no affiliate found. This is a direct-to-SMB SaaS with no channel program [high] (https://ai-receptionist.com/pricing/ ; /white-label/ 404 ; /reseller/ 404 ; /partners/ 404)
3 phone numbers: Both US AND Canada provisionable — dedicated Canada page confirms "Canadian phone numbers available"; site region selector lists US/Canada/UK-EU; "pick your own phone number or forward your existing number" [medium-high] (https://ai-receptionist.com/en-ca/)
4 call forwarding + porting: Forwarding YES ("carrier supported forwarding options"); number PORTING not mentioned anywhere on site/help — treat as unconfirmed/likely unsupported [medium] (https://ai-receptionist.com/features/)
5 calendar booking: Google Calendar YES (appointment scheduling on Starter+); Calendly / Cal.com NOT mentioned [high for Google; others UNKNOWN] (https://ai-receptionist.com/pricing/)
6 SMS/email lead summary: "System Emails" send automated alerts based on call triggers + an "SMS Communication Channel"; call transcripts included; an explicit post-call lead-summary is plausible but not verbatim-stated [medium] (https://ai-receptionist.com/features/)
7 CRM/Sheets/Zapier/webhooks: Weak — SIP/VoIP integration (3CX, FreePBX, Cisco, RingCentral) on Business plan; "Advanced CRM Integration (HubSpot, Salesforce)" listed as COMING SOON; no Zapier, webhooks, or Google Sheets mentioned [medium] (https://ai-receptionist.com/features/)
8 Stripe/client sub-account billing: NOT available — no reseller/sub-account/white-label billing; 0x4d2 LLC bills the end business directly by card; WE cannot bill clients through the platform [high, inferred from absence + no reseller program] (https://ai-receptionist.com/legal/cancellation-policy/)
9 setup time: Marketing claims "get up and running in minutes"; personalized-demo setup "within 1 business day" — realistic single-client launch likely under a day [medium, vendor marketing] (https://ai-receptionist.com/demo/)
10 demo quality: STRONG and ungated — 3 live public demo lines (Crank Sisters Bikes +1 206-888-4745, Black Drip Donuts +1 339-675-1477, Haunted House +1 229-715-9127) plus sample recordings/videos; no form wall [high] (https://ai-receptionist.com/demo/)
11 cancellation risk: Low lock-in but buyer-unfriendly — "cancel anytime, no contracts," monthly or annual, BUT "we do not provide refunds for cancellations"; account data permanently deleted 30 days after expiry. Third-party signal THIN: Trustpilot has only ~4 (positive) reviews and blocked direct fetch (403) — little independent validation [high for policy / low for third-party] (https://ai-receptionist.com/legal/cancellation-policy/)
12 hidden per-minute/overage: YES, material — included minutes are low (60 min on both the $14 and $39 plans; 200/400 on higher tiers); overage via "Booster Pack" at $0.25/minute (never expire); transferred minutes, outbound calls & SMS billed at "market rates" (~$0.01–$0.04 per call/segment), inbound MMS $0.04/image [high] (https://ai-receptionist.com/pricing/)
13 resell under OUR brand: NOT POSSIBLE as reseller or full white-label — no white-label/reseller/agency AND no affiliate/referral program exists; the product is sold and billed only under the "AI Receptionist" brand by 0x4d2 LLC. Best achievable today = informal manual referral (no rev-share program documented). Cannot rebrand as SourceA/Noetfield, cannot own the client billing relationship [high] (https://ai-receptionist.com/pricing/ ; /white-label/ 404)
suppliers.research_red_flags: CRUX FAILURE for our use case: no white-label, reseller, agency, OR affiliate program exists (/white-label/, /reseller/, /partners/ all 404; nothing in help center) — we cannot sell this under the SourceA/Noetfield brand. No client sub-account billing: 0x4d2 LLC bills each business directly, so we cannot own the billing relationship or set our own margin. Thin usage allowances (60 min on the $14 and $39 plans) plus $0.25/min overage and per-segment SMS/MMS "market rate" fees create hidden-cost/margin risk for any service we'd wrap around it. Key workflow integrations weak: HubSpot/Salesforce CRM is "coming soon," and there is no Zapier/webhooks/Google Sheets, limiting lead-routing to our own systems. Vendor scale/stability risk: operating entity is a small 2-founder LLC (0x4d2 LLC) that appears to be a side venture of Knowm Inc. (neuromorphic-computing research). Very small independent review footprint (~4 Trustpilot reviews; Trustpilot blocked our fetch) — low third-party validation of reliability. No refunds policy, and number porting is undocumented. Net: unsuitable as a resold/white-label offer; at most an informal referral relationship, which does not meet the "resell under our brand in 7 days" requirement.

verify.summary: Adversarial verification could NOT refute any deal-breaker claim; one was hardened. (a) PRICING is real, published, self-serve — independently confirmed exactly as reported: Essential $14/mo ($140/yr), Starter $39/mo ($399/yr), Solopreneur $99/mo ($999/yr), Business $199/mo ($1999/yr); 3-day trial, no setup fees; Enterprise unpriced. Overage is real and material: Booster Pack $0.25/min, outbound/SMS/MMS at "market rates," inbound MMS $0.04/image, on thin 60-min base allowances — hidden-cost risk HIGH. (b) WHITE-LABEL: none exists — /white-label/, /reseller/, /partners/ all return live HTTP 404; every affiliate/reseller/partner web search surfaced only COMPETITORS (My AI Front Desk, Trillet, Newo, etc.), never ai-receptionist.com or "0x4d2"; the unpriced Enterprise tier lists SLA/volume discounts but NO rebranding. There is no full white-label AND no affiliate/referral program to be mislabeled — it is absent entirely. (c) RESELL UNDER OUR BRAND: NO — and STRONGER than the original finding: the Terms of Service contain an explicit anti-resale clause — "You agree not to reproduce, duplicate, copy, sell, resell or exploit any portion of the Services... without the express written permission of the Company." 0x4d2 LLC bills the end business directly by card; no client sub-account/Stripe-Connect billing exists. Reselling is contractually prohibited by default; the only theoretical path is a bespoke written-permission/Enterprise contract (unpriced, un-standable in 7 days, no evidence rebranding is even offered). (d) THIRD-PARTY SIGNAL is genuinely THIN, not refutable: ~4 Trustpilot reviews (4-star), 0 Slashdot reviews, and the vendor's own /reviews/ page just mirrors the same 4 Trustpilot entries; entity founded 2024, small 2-founder side venture of Knowm Inc. No independent complaints found — but also almost no independent validation of reliability/outages. (e) 7-DAY LAUNCH under OUR brand = NO: technically a single client under THEIR brand could go live in ~a day (ungated live demo lines, "5-minute"/"1 business day" setup), but the requirement is resell under our brand, which is blocked by both the absence of white-label and the explicit anti-resale ToS clause. NET: unsuitable as a resold/white-label offer; the deal-breaker findings stand and the resell verdict is if anything firmer than reported.
red_flags: Explicit anti-resale clause in Terms of Service: 'You agree not to reproduce, duplicate, copy, sell, resell or exploit any portion of the Services... without the express written permission of the Company' — reselling under our brand is contractually prohibited by default (stronger than the original finding)

No white-label, reseller, agency, OR affiliate program exists: /white-label/, /reseller/, /partners/ all HTTP 404; every partner/affiliate search returned only competitors, never this vendor; Enterprise tier offers no rebranding

Cannot own the billing relationship: 0x4d2 LLC bills each end business directly by card; no client sub-account / Stripe-Connect / white-label billing — we cannot set our own price/margin through the platform
Material hidden usage costs: base allowances only 60 min on the $14 and $39 plans; $0.25/min Booster Pack overage; outbound calls, SMS, transferred minutes at 'market rates'; inbound MMS $0.04/image

Very thin independent validation: ~4 Trustpilot reviews (Trustpilot blocked direct fetch, 403), 0 Slashdot reviews, vendor 'reviews' page only re-displays the same 4 Trustpilot entries; low third-party proof of reliability/outages
Vendor scale/stability risk: small 2-founder LLC (0x4d2, founded 2024) that appears to be a side venture of Knowm Inc. (neuromorphic-computing research)
Buyer-unfriendly terms: ToS states they 'generally do not offer refunds' (may consider requests); account data permanently deleted 30 days after expiry
Key workflow integrations weak: HubSpot/Salesforce CRM listed 'coming soon'; no Zapier, webhooks, or Google Sheets; number PORTING undocumented (forwarding only)

7-day resell-under-our-brand requirement fails on two independent grounds (no white-label capability + anti-resale ToS), even though a single client under the vendor's own brand could launch in ~1 day

suppliers.label: Trillet
suppliers.findings:
1 pricing: D2C $49/mo (150 min, then $0.20/min); Studio $99/mo (100 min incl, 3 phone #s, 3 workspaces, +$15/extra workspace, +$5/mo/extra #, $0.12/min overage); Agency $299/mo (300 min incl, 10 phone #s, unlimited workspaces, custom minute markup, $0.12/min); Enterprise custom. Billed monthly; annual = 20% off; "no contracts, cancel anytime." [high] (https://trillet.ai/pricing , https://trillet.ai/plans)
2 white_label/reseller: REAL white-label reseller. Studio + Agency are white-label; Agency = custom domain, full branding, custom-branded emails, "the client never sees Trillet's brand." Separate 40% referral commission on referred agencies also exists (distinct affiliate layer). [high] (https://trillet.ai/pricing , https://www.trillet.ai/blogs/vapi-alternative-for-agencies)
3 phone_numbers CA+US: Native telephony (no separate Twilio account needed). US = yes (porting across major US carriers). CANADA = NOT explicitly supported — Trillet states porting is "strongest in the US and Australia" and to "confirm number availability... for those countries directly before committing"; vendor appears Australia-based (ACMA/DNCR compliance). Canadian DIDs UNCONFIRMED - must verify with vendor before launch. [high on the gap] (https://www.trillet.ai/blogs/white-label-ai-telephony-requirements)
4 call_forwarding+porting: Call forwarding = yes (~30-sec setup, keep existing number, configurable/conditional forwarding rules). Porting = US + Australia, 7-10 business days; Canada porting not mentioned. [high] (https://www.trillet.ai/agent , https://www.trillet.ai/blogs/white-label-ai-telephony-requirements)
5 calendar_booking: Google Calendar, Calendly, and Cal.com all listed as supported for appointment booking. [medium - from /agent page + cal.com ref on landing] (https://www.trillet.ai/agent)
6 sms_email_summary: Yes. Every call summarized (caller name, number, reason for calling, appointments booked) via email; SMS follow-up sends booking confirmations; multi-channel Voice/SMS/WhatsApp. [high] (https://www.trillet.ai/agent)
7 crm_zapier_webhooks: CRM integration = yes (live CRM read/write on landing page); Zapier = listed as supported; Webhooks = documented but "coming soon" in API reference. Google Sheets not explicitly confirmed. [medium] (https://www.trillet.ai/agent , https://docs.trillet.ai/v1/api-reference/introduction)
8 stripe_client_billing: Trillet's own blog claims "Native Stripe billing for automated client invoicing" plus "custom minute markup" on Agency plan — i.e., WE can rebill our clients. Caveat: vendor marketing claim, not on the pricing page and not independently verified. [medium] (https://www.trillet.ai/blogs/vapi-alternative-for-agencies , https://trillet.ai/pricing)
9 setup_time: Fast — "under 5 minutes / live within minutes": paste client's website URL, AI scrapes site + reviews to build a trained agent; call forwarding ~30 seconds. [high] (https://www.trillet.ai/agent , https://trillet.ai/blogs/best-white-label-ai-receptionist-for-agencies-2026)
10 demo_quality: Real call recordings/examples by industry + "live demo calls" on site; NO public demo phone number to dial. In lieu of a trial, 28-day money-back on the production product on your own number. [medium] (https://www.trillet.ai/agent , https://trillet.ai/blogs/ai-receptionist-free-trial-guide)
11 cancellation_risk: LOW-ish per vendor — "no contracts, cancel anytime," 28-day money-back guarantee (full refund, no questions) on all plans; annual billing (20% off) is the only lock-in if chosen. Third-party negative signal THIN: Trustpilot ~20 reviews, predominantly positive (praised white-label, telephony, voices, support "Andrew & Ming," rated above VAPI/Retell); no G2/Reddit complaints found; Scamadviser trust score 76. [medium] (https://trillet.ai/blogs/ai-receptionist-free-trial-guide , https://www.trustpilot.com/review/trillet.ai)
12 hidden_per_minute_cost: $0.12/min overage on Studio/Agency after included minutes; $0.20/min on D2C plan; extra phone numbers $5/mo; extra Studio workspaces +$15/mo. Included minutes are LOW (100 Studio / 300 Agency) so a busy receptionist client overruns fast — overage is the real cost driver; blog margin math (~85%) is vendor-optimistic. [high] (https://trillet.ai/pricing , https://trillet.ai/plans)
13 resell_under_our_brand: FULL WHITE-LABEL RESALE (not mere affiliate) available on Studio $99 and esp. Agency $299 — own domain/logo/branded emails, unlimited sub-accounts, custom minute markup, claimed native Stripe rebilling → we sell as SourceA/Noetfield. A separate 40% referral/affiliate program exists but is distinct from resale. [high] (https://trillet.ai/pricing , https://www.trillet.ai/blogs/vapi-alternative-for-agencies)
suppliers.research_red_flags: 1) CANADA PHONE NUMBERS UNCONFIRMED — biggest risk for a Canada+US launch: Trillet explicitly says porting/coverage is "strongest in the US and Australia" and to confirm other countries directly; vendor appears Australia-based (ACMA/DNCR). US is fine; Canadian DID provisioning + porting must be confirmed with the vendor BEFORE committing to a 7-day launch. If Canada is unsupported, half the target market is blocked. 2) Most feature/pricing detail comes from Trillet's OWN SEO blog network, not independent sources — Stripe client-rebilling, Zapier, and calendar claims are vendor-stated, not hands-on verified. 3) Webhooks listed as "coming soon" in the API docs — may not be production-ready. 4) Low included minutes (100/300) mean per-minute overage ($0.12) drives real cost; blog profit-margin math is optimistic. 5) Thin third-party track record (~20 Trustpilot reviews, no G2/Reddit; Scamadviser 76) — young vendor, limited longevity/reliability signal; Trustpilot page returned 403 on direct fetch (summary via search only). 6) No public demo phone number to dial before buying (evaluation is via 28-day money-back on your own number).

red_flags: Canada porting unsupported.
Integration claims are vendor-stated only.

suppliers.verify_status: FAILED — verifier returned placeholder; treat as research-only (NOT adversarially verified)

suppliers.label: NextPhone/Synthflow
suppliers.vendor: Synthflow (White-Label / Agency) — RECOMMENDED resell platform; NextPhone (getnextphone.com) was evaluated but is NOT white-label/resellable
suppliers.findings:
1 pricing: Synthflow official /pricing now shows only Enterprise — "Enterprise contracts start at $30,000 annually" (annual, sales-scoped by volume/telephony/integrations); legacy self-serve Agency plan ~$1,400/mo is retired for new signups per third-party; a Pro tier example is "$450/mo for 2,000 min." For contrast, NextPhone is a single flat $199/mo unlimited plan with 7-day trial. [medium] (https://synthflow.ai/pricing ; https://autocalls.ai/article/synthflow-review ; https://www.getnextphone.com/blog/ai-receptionist-cost)
2 white_label/reseller: Synthflow = TRUE full white-label reseller — "The Agency Plan includes full white labelling... the platform your clients log into looks like your product, not a third party tool." NextPhone = NO white-label/reseller/partner program on its homepage or pricing (only a vague non-official blog "$300–$500/month premium" mention). [high] (https://synthflow.ai/blog/white-label-reseller ; https://www.getnextphone.com/ ; https://www.getnextphone.com/blog/ai-receptionist-cost)
3 phone numbers: Synthflow directly provisions US AND Canadian numbers (also Australia) at ~$1.50/mo, plus BYO via Twilio/Telnyx/RingCentral — Canada + US both covered. [high] (https://docs.synthflow.ai/docs/buy-a-phone-number-with-synthflow)
4 call forwarding + porting: Synthflow "supports number porting, forwarding, and SIP URI targeting" — both yes. [high] (https://docs.synthflow.ai/about-telephony)
5 calendar booking: Synthflow enterprise feature list includes native "calendar" integration (Google Calendar / Cal.com commonly cited) plus Zapier bridge; not a single quoted Calendly line. [medium] (https://synthflow.ai/pricing)
6 sms_email_lead_summary: NOT confirmed as a named native Synthflow feature — achievable via post-call webhooks / Zapier / Make. (NextPhone by contrast natively sends "call summaries, transcripts, and recordings via SMS or email.") [low for Synthflow / high for NextPhone] (https://synthflow.ai/pricing ; https://www.getnextphone.com/)
7 crm_sheets_zapier_webhooks: Synthflow enterprise lists "CRM, calendar, contact-center stack, webhooks, API"; also GoHighLevel embed + Zapier — Sheets reachable via Zapier. [high] (https://synthflow.ai/pricing ; https://synthflow.ai/blog/white-label-reseller)
8 stripe_client_subaccount_billing: YES — "Each client sits in its own sub-account... allocate call minutes individually" and rebilling "automates billing at [your client-facing] prices, so your margin is built in." We can bill clients directly. [high] (https://synthflow.ai/blog/white-label-reseller)
9 setup_time_one_client: Not officially stated for white-label; it is a self-serve agent-builder + sub-account model (you configure the agent), so realistically a few days — a 7-day launch is feasible but NOT turnkey like NextPhone's stated "live in 1–2 days." [low] (https://synthflow.ai/blog/white-label-reseller ; https://www.getnextphone.com/)
10 demo_quality: Self-serve builder lets you build/test an agent; otherwise "Contact Sales / request a demo." No public live-call demo phone number confirmed. [low] (https://synthflow.ai/pricing)
11 cancellation_risk: Enterprise is ANNUAL "$30,000" contracts = real lock-in; legacy Agency was month-to-month. No refund policy published; third-party review did not state cancellation terms. [medium] (https://synthflow.ai/pricing ; https://autocalls.ai/article/synthflow-review)
12 hidden_per_minute_overage: MAJOR — BYOK model; advertised per-minute excludes provider fees. Third-party: platform $0.09–$0.23/min + ElevenLabs/LLM/Deepgram/Twilio push real all-in to $0.15–$0.37/min; "Real cost is 2–3× the advertised rate." [medium-high, third-party] (https://autocalls.ai/article/synthflow-review)
13 resell_under_our_brand: Synthflow = FULL WHITE-LABEL (rebranded dashboard as SourceA/Noetfield + per-client sub-accounts + rebilling) = true resale, not affiliate. Note limitation: page mentions logo/branding, does NOT confirm custom-domain. NextPhone offers no resale path at all (affiliate-or-nothing). [high] (https://synthflow.ai/blog/white-label-reseller ; https://www.getnextphone.com/)
suppliers.research_red_flags:
NEXTPHONE: Not resellable — no white-label/reseller/partner program on homepage or /pricing (only an unofficial blog "$300–$500 premium" mention); it is an end-customer product, so resell_verdict for NextPhone itself = NO. Homepage hides the price (blog reveals flat $199/mo). Blog network reads SEO-heavy; ZERO independent NextPhone reviews found on G2/Trustpilot/Reddit (only a look-alike nextphones.de) — low third-party validation.
SYNTHFLOW (recommended): (1) Real white-label is now gated behind Enterprise ANNUAL contracts "start at $30,000" — self-serve Agency (~$1,400/mo) reportedly retired for new signups, so cheap entry is uncertain/sales-gated. (2) BYOK hidden per-minute: advertised rates exclude ElevenLabs/LLM/STT/Twilio; real all-in $0.15–$0.37/min, "2–3× advertised" (third-party) — margins can evaporate on a flat-fee resale. (3) Custom DOMAIN not confirmed (only logo/dashboard branding), which weakens "fully our brand." (4) SMS/email post-call lead summary is not a named native feature (needs Zapier/webhooks). (5) Heavy fixed cost before first client ($1,400–$2,000+/mo legacy, or $30k/yr enterprise) is aggressive for a 7-day budget launch. 
CHEAPER-BUT-GATED ALT: Dialzara publishes low self-serve plans ($29/$99/$199/$349 + $0.35–$0.48/min overage) and a Partner Program, but its white-label/reseller terms are "Custom — contact sales" = UNKNOWN, so it could not be verified for resale in time. 
VERDICT NUANCE: Synthflow's white-label MECHANICS (sub-accounts + rebilling + US/Canada numbers + porting + CRM/webhooks) fit our resale model strongly; the "possible" (not strong-fit) rating is driven by high/partly-sales-gated cost, BYOK per-minute exposure, and unconfirmed custom-domain — validate exact white-label price and per-minute all-in with Synthflow sales before committing to the 7-day launch.

verify.summary: Adversarial verification largely UPHOLDS the core recommendation but tightens the risk picture. WHITE-LABEL (confirmed, even stronger than the finding): Synthflow is a genuine full white-label reseller, not affiliate mislabeled — official blog + docs confirm own-logo dashboard "clients log into looks like your product," per-client sub-accounts, and Stripe rebilling where you set your own client-facing prices with built-in margin and configurable per-minute overage. Notably, the finding's caveat #3 ("custom DOMAIN not confirmed") is OVERTURNED: Synthflow's own docs (docs.synthflow.ai/about-agency-whitelabel) state "host your whitelabel app on your own domain." No anti-resale clause found. RESELL UNDER OUR BRAND: yes — we can bill our own clients at our own prices via Stripe sub-accounts. PRICING (partial / mostly sales-gated): Synthflow's official /pricing now publishes ONLY "Enterprise contracts start at $30,000 annually" — a floor, not a firm quote. The cheaper self-serve white-label path (Pay-As-You-Go + ~$2,000/mo white-label add-on) appears only on third-party aggregators (checkthat.ai), NOT on Synthflow's own pricing page; legacy self-serve Pro/Growth/Agency tiers ($450/$900/$1,400/mo) are confirmed RETIRED for new signups. So exact white-label entry cost is unconfirmed and must be quoted by sales. HIDDEN COST (high): confirmed BYOK model — advertised per-minute excludes ElevenLabs/LLM/Deepgram/Twilio; third-party review adds $0.07–$0.16/min on top of $0.09–$0.23 platform (Pro example $590–$770 real vs $450 advertised; review calls "real cost 2–3x advertised" the "#1 complaint"). LOCK-IN/BILLING (worse than the finding stated): real Trustpilot/G2 reviewers report being charged 5+ months AFTER cancelling, unable to reach a human, forced to cancel their card; plus "bait-and-switch" complaints that the useful tooling is gated behind the overpriced enterprise tier. NEXTPHONE claim holds: confirmed NON-resellable — only a 25%/12-month affiliate referral program (Pure Labs Inc), no white-label/custom-brand path, and thin independent validation. 7-DAY LAUNCH (maybe): self-serve build is genuinely fast (reviews praise no-code setup), but if white-label is only obtainable via the $30k annual enterprise contract, the sales cycle + annual commitment makes a 7-day budget launch questionable; feasible only if the ~$2,000/mo self-serve add-on path is confirmed real. NET: the recommendation direction (Synthflow over NextPhone for reselling under our own brand) is sound and the white-label mechanics fit our resale model strongly; but treat white-label price as sales-gated, budget for BYOK per-minute roughly doubling advertised rates, and get cancellation/refund terms in writing before committing.
red_flags: White-label cost is effectively SALES-GATED: Synthflow's official /pricing publishes only 'Enterprise contracts start at $30,000 annually' (a floor). The cheaper self-serve path (PAYG + ~$2,000/mo white-label add-on) is reported ONLY by third-party aggregators, not on Synthflow's own site; legacy $1,400/mo Agency is confirmed retired for new signups. Get the exact white-label price in writing from sales.

Hidden BYOK per-minute overage (confirmed): advertised per-minute EXCLUDES ElevenLabs/LLM/Deepgram/Twilio. Third-party review adds ~$0.07–$0.16/min on top of $0.09–$0.23 platform (Pro example: $590–$770 real vs $450 advertised); characterized as 'real cost 2–3x advertised' and the '#1 complaint.' A flat-fee resale can lose money on heavy-usage clients.

Cancellation/continued-billing risk (NEW, worse than finding): real Trustpilot/G2 reviews report being billed 5+ months after cancelling, no reachable human for support, and having to cancel their debit card to stop charges. Directly contradicts a low cancellation-risk read.
'Bait-and-switch' complaints: multiple reviewers say the genuinely useful tooling is locked behind the overpriced enterprise plan — relevant because white-label itself sits in that gated tier.
Enterprise route is an ANNUAL commitment (~$30k floor) with no published refund policy = real lock-in and heavy fixed cost before the first client; aggressive for a 7-day budget launch.
Quality/latency inconsistency noted across reviews (can feel like a 'polished IVR' on unpredictable/conversational calls) — validate live before selling to clients.

NextPhone confirmed NON-resellable: only a 25%/12-month affiliate referral program (operated by Pure Labs Inc), no white-label/custom-brand/sub-account path; also thin independent third-party validation. Confirms it is an end-customer product, not a resale platform.

Custom-domain caveat OVERTURNED (in Synthflow's favor): official docs confirm own-domain hosting, so the finding was too pessimistic there — but do verify domain + branding in a live demo/sandbox before committing.
